title: jiff Integration
summary: Goal: Expose the jiff datetime crate to R via a new jiff optional feature in miniextendr-api. Branch: feat/jiff-integration Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-22-jiff-integration-design.md Review: reviews/2026-04-22-jiff-integration.md

title: Plan: Fix All Audit Issues
cargo-revendor test matrix
summary: Gap analysis vs the existing 23 integration tests in cargo-revendor/tests/integration.rs, plus a harness proposal for elaborate multi-workspace scenarios.

title: Comprehensive condition system tests + tail of #344/#349 cleanup
summary: PR #344 landed error! / warning! / message! / condition! macros with rust* class layering and a tagged-SEXP transport. PR #349 extended layering to ALTREP RUnwind callbacks for the Error variant. PR #382 hoisted the per-wrapper switch into .miniextendrraise_condition. This plan closes the remaining test surface gaps and the one known correctness gap (#366), and renames the transport class now that it carries non-error conditions too.

title: Condition system unification: #316 + #319 + #317 + Rf_error doc cleanup
summary: Goal: Fold three pending issues + a doc-correctness cleanup into one coherent PR. Result: a single, well-documented Rust→R condition pipeline with structured rust_ class layering for every* signal Rust raises (errors, warnings, messages, conditions), correct call attribution at every site, and honest docs.

title: Plan: Full DataFusion integration (SQL + DataFrame API)
summary: Extend the current thin datafusion feature (RSessionContext with block_on) into a complete query interface: register R data, run SQL, use DataFrame API, read/write files.

title: Plan: Create `tools/detect-features.R` for configure-time feature detection
summary: rpkg/configure.ac references tools/detect-features.R for auto-detecting which cargo features to enable, but the file doesn't exist. Currently falls back to a hardcoded list of all features. Create the script so scaffolded packages can auto-detect available features.

title: Plan: Extract Optional Integrations into `miniextendr-*` Crates (No Cycles)
summary: Move optional integration logic out of miniextendr-api into dedicated crates while keeping miniextendr-api as the user-facing R boundary.

title: Plan: Fix class-system-matrix test failures (13 remaining)
summary: Types with #[miniextendr] (Env) inherent impl + different-class-system trait impl don't get $.ClassName generated. The $ dispatch method is only generated when the inherent impl codegen runs for env-class — but the class-system-matrix types split inherent and trait impls across different class systems.

title: Flat local-crate vendor paths (hybrid layout)
summary: Issue #284. Option 2: cargo-revendor --freeze writes flat vendor/<name>/ paths for local workspace crates (miniextendr-api, miniextendr-macros, miniextendr-lint, etc.) while keeping cargo vendor --versioned-dirs for transitive deps. minirextendr already expects this layout on both the read and write side — only cargo-revendor drifted.
title = "Issue #112 — FFI encapsulation: scalar constructors (cat 4) + ALTREP data slots (cat 6)" +++

title: issue-243: Retarget standalone-fn codegen at CWrapperContext
summary: Issue: #243 (deferred #19 from the macros-refactor sweep)

title: Issue #248 — reject Self-returning vctrs constructors
summary: miniextendr-macros/src/miniextendrimpl/vctrsclass.rs generates an R wrapper that always passes .val (the C-call result) to vctrs::newvctr(). When the impl method returns Self, .val is an EXTPTRSXP (Rust Self handle) — invalid for newvctr, which errors .data must be a vector type.
title = "cargo-revendor: --external-only / --local-only phase modes (#290)" description = "Split vendoring pipeline into two independent phase modes for CI cacheability" +++

title: issue-307 — `ColumnarDataFrame`: all-`None` columns land as logical NA, not list-of-NULL
summary: Tracks https://github.com/A2-ai/miniextendr/issues/307.

title: Issue #318 — `call_attribution_demo` fixture pollutes public namespace
summary: Apply option 1 from the issue (@keywords internal), not option 3 (move to testthat). Keep the doc transcript in docs/CALLATTRIBUTION.md runnable but require miniextendr:::callattr_with(...) (triple-colon prefix).

title: Issue #343 — extract paragraphs 3+ as @details
summary: miniextendr-macros/src/roxygen.rs:355 implicitdescriptionfrom_attrs extracts only the second paragraph and breaks at the first blank line within it. Paragraphs 3+ are dropped before the first @tag.

title = "Trait-ABI + ALTREP: route panics through tagged-SEXP for rust* class layering (#345)" description = "Make panics from cross-package trait method calls and ALTREP RUnwind callbacks match tryCatch(rusterror = h, ...)" +++
title = "Issue #363 — Unify makerusterrorvalue into makerustconditionvalue" +++

title: Issue #379 — S7 @prop emission for sidecar (r_data_accessors) properties
summary: Add propdoc slot on #[rdataaccessors(propdoc = "...")]. ExternalPtr derive macro emits compile-time MXS7SIDECARPROPS distributedslice entries. S7 codegen reads them at write time, folds into the @prop loop.

title: Issue #384 — declare R (>= 4.4) for `%||%` in generated wrappers
summary: Add Depends: R (>= 4.4) to rpkg/DESCRIPTION. Convention: place it just after the Type: field or between Title: and Authors@R:, matching how other CRAN packages format it.

title: Issue #411 — declare R (>= 4.4) for cross-package DESCRIPTIONs
summary: Add Depends: R (>= 4.4) to:

summary: Generate the protocol-method R wrapper as:

title: Plan: `Lazy<T>` — Opt-in ALTREP lazy materialization for all conversion types
summary: Introduce Lazy<T> as an opt-in wrapper that returns Rust data to R as ALTREP vectors instead of copying. R reads elements on demand; full materialization only happens if R needs a contiguous pointer (e.g., for .Internal(inspect(x))).

title: Git LFS History Cleanup
summary: Remove all historical copies of vendor.tar.xz from git objects across all branches. Currently 54 versions totaling ~408 MB bloat the repo.

title: Lockfile + configure + minirextendr unification
summary: > Status (2026-05-07): DONE. All nine items shipped between PR #400 and > #408 (see Related at bottom for the full mapping). Item 8 > ([[patch.unused]] rule) was added to lock-shape-check.R, the > just lock-shape-check recipe, and both pre-commit hooks in the > close-out PR; the same PR removed the obsolete checksum =  block from > rpkg/tools/lock-shape-check.R (and the minirextendr template + scaffold > hook) that #408 forgot to update. This file is kept as historical > reference.

title: miniextendr-macros refactor
summary: Branch: review/macros-refactor

title = "minirextendr R CMD check cleanup" description = "Fix three pre-existing R CMD check lints in minirextendr: non-ASCII characters, undeclared test dependencies, and unqualified head() calls." +++

title: MXL110: R reserved words as `#[miniextendr]` parameter names
summary: Tracks: #386. Found while writing comprehensive condition tests in PR #385: a pub fn foo(repeat: i32) fixture compiled cleanly via cargo build but produced conditionerrorlong_message <- function(repeat in the generated wrappers file, which devtools::document() rejected with a syntax error several minutes into the build.

title: MXL111: redundant `s4_` prefix on `#[miniextendr(s4)]` method names
summary: Tracks: #387. Found while writing S4 condition fixtures in PR #385: a method named s4raiseerror on an #[miniextendr(s4)] impl produced an R generic named s4s4raiseerror, because the S4 codegen unconditionally prepends s4 to method names.

title: Plan: Builder-First Parallel Column Chunks for DataFrames
summary: Replace a closure-only API with a builder-style API that feels like itertools and Rayon:

title: Follow-ups from PR #382 review (`refactor/raise-condition-helper`)
summary: PR #382 hoists the per-wrapper switch(.val$kind, ...) block into a single .miniextendrraisecondition helper emitted at the top of the generated wrappers file. Net result: rpkg/R/miniextendr-wrappers.R shrinks 43,480 → 24,658 lines (-43%).

title: Plan: Scope-as-Allocator (Protected-by-Construction)
summary: Builds on unified-gc-protection.md. That plan adds Protector trait + pool. This plan changes the API surface: instead of allocate-then-protect, the scope IS the allocator.

title: Plan: Unified GC Protection API
summary: Background: analysis/gc-protection-strategies.md

title: Plan: Vendor Examples as R Test Fixtures
summary: Implement upstream crate examples as R test fixtures in rpkg, organized by optional integration crate. Each item is a new Rust #[miniextendr] function in the corresponding rpkg/src/rust/*adaptertests.rs file, plus an R test in rpkg/tests/testthat/.

title: Vendor only inside R CMD build → R CMD INSTALL <tarball>
summary: Vendoring (rpkg/vendor/ + rpkg/inst/vendor.tar.xz) becomes a CRAN-tarball-only artifact.

title = "Vendoring: full lifecycle review + test plan" description = "Exhaustive review and test plan for the entire vendoring system: cargo-revendor, configure.ac, bootstrap.R, Makevars.in, templates, minirextendr, CI, and every user/install pathway" +++

title: WASM registry codegen — eliminating linkme on `wasm32-*`
summary: Companion to plans/webr-support.md (which sketches this at a high level). This plan goes deep on the mechanism: how we replace linkme's link-time gather with a host-time snapshot that gets compiled in on WASM.

title: WebR CI workflow
summary: Goal: a CI job that exercises the wasm32 build path end-to-end, gated on paths so docs-only PRs don't fire it. Optional. Steps 2–7 of webr-support.md are merged; everything compiles for wasm32-unknown-emscripten. This plan is what closes the loop on "reviewer can know wasm32 still works without running it locally."

title: `rpkg/configure.ac` wasm32 detection + `rpkg/build.rs` snapshot validation
summary: Two coupled rpkg-side compile-time concerns that close out the wasm32 install path. Both are step 8 of webr-support.md (split here for clarity).

title: Cross-package `wasm_registry.rs` stubs
summary: Status: not started. Low priority — tests/cross-package/{consumer,producer}.pkg are native-only trait-ABI test crates, never deployed to webR.

title: Dockerfile for miniextendr WebR builds
summary: Goal: a Dockerfile.webr (lives at repo root) that gives us — and CI — a reproducible environment for building & smoke-testing miniextendr against webR's WASM toolchain. Inherits the upstream webR image so we don't re-stage Emscripten, flang, Rust nightly, or R-for-WASM ourselves.

title: WebR / WASM support for miniextendr
summary: Goal: build miniextendr-api (and the rpkg miniextendr R package) for the wasm32-unknown-emscripten target, so it can be installed inside webR (R compiled to WASM via Emscripten).
